name: IBM
status_note: IBM remains an active global technology company as of 2026

name: Oracle
status_note: Oracle remains an active global database and cloud technology company as of 2026

name: Sybase
status_note: Sybase was acquired by SAP in July 2010 for approximately $5.8 billion; operates as SAP Sybase

name: Informix
status_note: Informix acquired by IBM in 2001 for $1 billion; continued as IBM Informix; active development delegated to HCLSoftware in 2017

name: Computer Associates
status_note: Computer Associates rebranded as CA Technologies; subsequently acquired by Broadcom in 2018

name: Transarc
status_note: Transarc (maker of Encina TP monitor) acquired by IBM in 1994

name: Novell
status_note: Novell (Tuxedo TP monitor) still operates; acquired by Attachmate in 2011, then Micro Focus in 2014

name: Seer Technologies
status_note: Third-party client-server application development environment (CADE) provider with close IBM ties; current status unknown

name: Lotus Development
status_note: Lotus acquired by IBM in 1995; Lotus Notes eventually folded into IBM Collaboration Solutions

name: Powersoft
status_note: Powersoft acquired by Sybase in 1995; PowerBuilder product line continues under SAP

name: Aberdeen Group
status_note: Aberdeen Group is an active technology research and analysis firm

name: Lotus Notes / Domino
status_note: IBM acquired Lotus in 1995; Notes/Domino sold to HCL in 2018 and remains actively developed
